skill.name: llm-evaluation
skill.description: Implement comprehensive evaluation strategies for LLM applications using automated metrics, human feedback, and benchmarking.
skill.summary: Systematic evaluation framework for measuring LLM performance with automated metrics, human evaluation, and statistical testing.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only containing Python code examples for LLM evaluation. All static analysis findings are false positives: Python code blocks were misidentified as Ruby/shell commands, and dictionary keys were incorrectly flagged as cryptographic operations. No executable code or security risks detected.

content.user_title: Evaluate LLM Applications with Comprehensive Metrics
content.value_statement: Measuring LLM performance is complex and error-prone. This skill provides systematic evaluation frameworks combining automated metrics, human judgment, and statistical testing to validate AI application quality.
actual_capabilities: Implement automated evaluation metrics including BLEU, ROUGE, BERTScore, and custom measures
Design human evaluation frameworks with annotation guidelines and inter-rater agreement
Apply LLM-as-Judge patterns for scalable quality assessment
Conduct A/B testing with statistical significance analysis
Build regression detection systems for CI/CD pipelines
Run standardized benchmarks across different models
limitations: Requires access to evaluation datasets and test cases for meaningful results
Human evaluation components need manual reviewer coordination
LLM-as-Judge patterns incur additional API costs and latency
Statistical significance requires sufficient sample sizes for valid conclusions

use_cases.title: ML Engineer Validating Model Changes
use_cases.description: Run comprehensive evaluation suites before deploying prompt or model updates to catch performance regressions early.

use_cases.title: Product Team Comparing AI Vendors
use_cases.description: Benchmark multiple LLM providers on domain-specific tasks to make data-driven vendor selection decisions.

use_cases.title: Research Team Publishing Results
use_cases.description: Generate statistically rigorous evaluation results with proper metrics and significance testing for academic publications.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Metric Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to evaluate an LLM that generates customer support responses. What metrics should I use and how do I implement them?

prompt_templates.title: Build Evaluation Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an evaluation suite for my RAG application that measures accuracy, groundedness, and retrieval quality. Include both automated and human evaluation components.

prompt_templates.title: A/B Test Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: I have evaluation scores from two prompt variants: Variant A [scores] and Variant B [scores]. Determine if the difference is statistically significant and calculate effect size.

prompt_templates.title: Production Evaluation Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a CI/CD integration that runs regression detection on every model update, alerts on performance drops above 5 percent, and generates comparison reports against baseline.

output_examples.input: Evaluate a summarization model using ROUGE metrics
output_examples.output: ROUGE-1: 0.72, ROUGE-2: 0.58, ROUGE-L: 0.65 - Strong performance on unigram overlap with moderate bigram coherence
Winner: Response B (confidence: 8/10). Response B provides more accurate citations and better structured arguments, though both answers address the core question adequately.

output_examples.input: Analyze A/B test results for statistical significance
output_examples.output: Variant B shows 12 percent improvement over A with p-value 0.03. Result is statistically significant at alpha=0.05 with medium effect size (Cohen's d=0.54).

best_practices: Use multiple complementary metrics rather than optimizing for a single score
Always establish baseline performance before measuring improvements
Combine automated metrics with human evaluation for comprehensive assessment
anti_patterns: Drawing conclusions from evaluation on too few test examples
Using evaluation metrics that do not align with business objectives
Testing on data that overlaps with training data (data contamination)

faq.question: What is the minimum sample size for reliable LLM evaluation?
faq.answer: For statistical significance testing, aim for at least 100 evaluation examples. For high-stakes decisions, 500-1000 examples provide more reliable results with narrower confidence intervals.

faq.question: How do I choose between automated metrics and human evaluation?
faq.answer: Use automated metrics for fast iteration and regression detection. Add human evaluation for final validation, especially when assessing subjective qualities like helpfulness, safety, or nuanced correctness.

faq.question: Can LLM-as-Judge replace human evaluators entirely?
faq.answer: LLM-as-Judge works well for routine quality checks and scales efficiently, but human evaluation remains essential for complex judgments, safety assessment, and validating the judge model itself.

faq.question: How often should I re-run evaluations on my LLM application?
faq.answer: Run evaluations on every code or prompt change as part of CI/CD. For production monitoring, run daily or weekly evaluations on fresh samples to detect drift or performance degradation.

faq.question: What should I do when metrics disagree with each other?
faq.answer: Metric disagreement often reveals trade-offs. Investigate which metric aligns best with your actual goals through error analysis, and consider using a weighted composite score reflecting business priorities.

faq.question: How do I evaluate multi-turn conversations?
faq.answer: Use conversation-level metrics like task completion rate and user satisfaction alongside turn-level metrics. Consider coherence across turns and whether the model maintains context appropriately throughout the dialogue.
